title: ellipanthus hemandradenioides
intro: Ellipanthus hemandradenioides, also known as the Red Stinging Plant or Red Stinging Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. It is a climber found in tropical Africa, known for its red, stinging hairs that can cause pain and inflammation. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations from Ellipanthus hemandradenioides include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to provide relief from pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and support overall health.
